description: Grass-like plants with stems that have edges, and flowers that are enclosed inside an inflated sac (perigynium)
Plants whose daisy-like "flowers" actually consist of two flowers - disks and rays

pile_friendly_name: Primitive plants in the genus Equisetum, lacking true leaves
description: Spore-bearing plants with firm, jointed branches, usually hollow stems, and cones

pile_friendly_name: Plants in the families Lycopodiaceae or Isoetaceae
description: Spore-bearing plants with small, scale-like or grass-like leaves

pile_friendly_name: Ferns, moonworts, and Adder's-tongues with obvious leaves
description: Spore-bearing plants with large, often lobed or compound leaves
Plants with flower parts numbering 3 or 6 mostly equal-sized parts

pile_friendly_name: Milfoils, watershields, bladderworts, and other submerged plants
description: Plants with their leaves and/or stems submerged or floating in water, only flowers (if anything) standing above the water surface (note:  if the plant has leaves that protrude above the surface of the water, then seek its identification in other piles)
Plants with showy, bilaterally symmetrical flowers with one part (the lip) often larger or more elaborate than the other petals

pile_friendly_name: Plants in the grass family, Poaceae
description: Plants with narrow leaves enclosing a hollow, rounded stem with a sheath at a swollen node

pile_friendly_name: Bulrushes, rushes, cat-tails, and other narrow-leaved plants
description: Grass-like plants without swollen stem nodes, that bear flowers in more than two ranks

pile_friendly_name: Duckweeds and other very small floating species
description: Floating plants that consist only of a small (10mm) body

pile_friendly_name: Trees, shrubs, and lianas that produce flowers and seeds with carpels
description: Woody plants with flat or broad leaves, which bear flowers and fruits

pile_friendly_name: Pines, spruces, larches, cedars, and other conifers
description: Woody plants with needle-like leaves, which bear their seeds in scaly or berry-like cones
